response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a capable full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is a poor fit for this role on every mandatory dimension. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years of professional Windows C++ development required. His SQL experience covers MySQL with no mention of SQL Server or Oracle. Most critically, he has zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any adjacent financial-services domain; his career spans web apps, GIS platforms, direct mail, and ad-tech. The optional desirables (Summit, STL, Kafka, Python for quant work) are also absent. There is no reasonable path to presenting this profile as a match.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps background (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development) covers one desirable skill area
Java development experience at Altom (robotic arms) and National Instruments satisfies the optional Java background
Demonstrated ability to work in low-latency, high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services at millions of req/s) shows performance-oriented engineering mindset
concerns: C++ experience is approximately six months (NI, 2014–2015) plus hobby/thesis work — far below the mandatory 5–10 year threshold, and no indication of Windows-platform C++ development
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL skills mentioned only generically (MySQL), with no database-heavy project descriptions relevant to capital markets data workloads
Zero financial-services domain knowledge — no capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or related exposure anywhere in a 15-year career
